spine.spine: Choose the boxes, then count: every result is the one counting fact, sharpened or applied.
spine.reasoning: One counting fact dominates the whole source. The simple form is a special case of the strong form; the worked examples (socks, birthdays, R(3,3)) and the Erdős–Szekeres result are not different tools but the same tool with a clever choice of boxes/labels, so every section is upstream or downstream of the one theorem. Runner-up was an engine-list of 'tricks', rejected because the tricks share one engine — single-theorem has strictly higher coverage and teaching impact, and the skill the source repeatedly names ('choosing the boxes') is exactly the spine.
key_concepts: strong form: some box holds at least ceil(n/k)
contrapositive + counting as the proof engine
section_order: The one idea, and how to read
The principle (strong form; simple form as a special case)
The non-obvious move — a clever map into a small label set
